summary: This is an extremely brief post where Joel Spolsky puts out a call on behalf of his publisher seeking an author to write a book about programming for accessibility. He asks qualified or interested people to contact him so he can connect them with the publisher. At just 34 words, this is essentially a classified ad rather than a substantive blog post.
key_points: Joel's publisher was actively seeking an author for a book on accessibility programming, suggesting industry interest in the topic circa 2000
Joel used his blog's readership as a recruiting channel for his publisher, demonstrating the platform's reach even in its early days
The post reflects that accessibility in software was recognized as a topic deserving dedicated book-length treatment at the turn of the millennium
money_quotes: My publisher is looking for someone to write a book about programming for accessibility.
key_insight: Even in 2000, accessibility in software development was recognized as an important enough topic to warrant a dedicated book, though finding qualified authors proved challenging enough to require a public call.
